lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਸਲ ਫਕੀਰਾਂ ਗਾਖੜੀ ਸੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਪੂਰ ਕਰੰਮਿ ॥੧੧੧॥
lines.translit: misal fakeeraan gaakharree su paaeeai poor karam |111|
lines.english: It is so difficult to be like the fakeers - the Holy Saints; it is only achieved by perfect karma. ||111||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਹਿਲੈ ਪਹਰੈ ਫੁਲੜਾ ਫਲੁ ਭੀ ਪਛਾ ਰਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: pahilai paharai fularraa fal bhee pachhaa raat |
lines.english: The first watch of the night brings flowers, and the later watches of the night bring fruit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਾਗੰਨੑਿ ਲਹੰਨਿ ਸੇ ਸਾਈ ਕੰਨੋ ਦਾਤਿ ॥੧੧੨॥
lines.translit: jo jaagana lahan se saaee kano daat |112|
lines.english: Those who remain awake and aware, receive the gifts from the Lord. ||112||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਤੀ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸੰਦੀਆ ਕਿਆ ਚਲੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: daatee saahib sandeea kiaa chalai tis naal |
lines.english: The gifts are from our Lord and Master; who can force Him to bestow them?

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਜਾਗੰਦੇ ਨਾ ਲਹਨੑਿ ਇਕਨੑਾ ਸੁਤਿਆ ਦੇਇ ਉਠਾਲਿ ॥੧੧੩॥
lines.translit: eik jaagande naa lehana ikanaa sutiaa dee utthaal |113|
lines.english: Some are awake, and do not receive them, while He awakens others from sleep to bless them. ||113||

lines.gurmukhi: ਢੂਢੇਦੀਏ ਸੁਹਾਗ ਕੂ ਤਉ ਤਨਿ ਕਾਈ ਕੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: dtoodtedee suhaag koo tau tan kaaee kor |
lines.english: You search for your Husband Lord; you must have some fault in your body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਰ ਮੰਝ ਕਮਾਣ ਏ ਸਬਰੁ ਕਾ ਨੀਹਣੋ ॥
lines.translit: sabar manjh kamaan e sabar kaa neehano |
lines.english: Within yourself, make patience the bow, and make patience the bowstring.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑਾ ਨਾਉ ਸੁਹਾਗਣੀ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਝਾਕ ਨ ਹੋਰ ॥੧੧੪॥
lines.translit: jinaa naau suhaaganee tinaa jhaak na hor |114|
lines.english: Those who are known as happy soul-brides, do not look to others. ||114||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਰ ਸੰਦਾ ਬਾਣੁ ਖਾਲਕੁ ਖਤਾ ਨ ਕਰੀ ॥੧੧੫॥
lines.translit: sabar sandaa baan khaalak khataa na karee |115|
lines.english: Make patience the arrow, the Creator will not let you miss the target. ||115||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਨਿ ਨਜੀਕਿ ਖੁਦਾਇ ਦੈ ਭੇਤੁ ਨ ਕਿਸੈ ਦੇਨਿ ॥੧੧੬॥
lines.translit: hon najeek khudaae dai bhet na kisai den |116|
lines.english: They are close to the Lord, but they do not reveal their secret to anyone. ||116||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਰ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਾਬਰੀ ਤਨੁ ਏਵੈ ਜਾਲੇਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: sabar andar saabaree tan evai jaalena |
lines.english: Those who are patient abide in patience; in this way, they burn their bodies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਧਿ ਥੀਵਹਿ ਦਰੀਆਉ ਟੁਟਿ ਨ ਥੀਵਹਿ ਵਾਹੜਾ ॥੧੧੭॥
lines.translit: vadh theeveh dareeaau ttutt na theeveh vaaharraa |117|
lines.english: In this way, you will grow into a great river; you will not break off into a tiny stream. ||117||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਰੁ ਏਹੁ ਸੁਆਉ ਜੇ ਤੂੰ ਬੰਦਾ ਦਿੜੁ ਕਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: sabar ehu suaau je toon bandaa dirr kareh |
lines.english: Let patience be your purpose in life; implant this within your being.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਿ ਕਿਨੈ ਚਾਲੀਐ ਦਰਵੇਸਾਵੀ ਰੀਤਿ ॥੧੧੮॥
lines.translit: eikan kinai chaaleeai daravesaavee reet |118|
lines.english: Only a rare few follow the way of the Saints. ||118||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦਰਵੇਸੀ ਗਾਖੜੀ ਚੋਪੜੀ ਪਰੀਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa daravesee gaakharree choparree pareet |
lines.english: Fareed, it is difficult to be a dervish - a Holy Saint; it is easier to love bread when it is buttered.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਤਪੈ ਤਨੂਰ ਜਿਉ ਬਾਲਣੁ ਹਡ ਬਲੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: tan tapai tanoor jiau baalan hadd balana |
lines.english: My body is cooking like an oven; my bones are burning like firewood.

lines.translit: pairee thakaan sir julaan je moon piree milana |119|
lines.english: If my feet become tired, I will walk on my head, if I can meet my Beloved. ||119||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਨ ਤਪਾਇ ਤਨੂਰ ਜਿਉ ਬਾਲਣੁ ਹਡ ਨ ਬਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: tan na tapaae tanoor jiau baalan hadd na baal |
lines.english: Do not heat up your body like an oven, and do not burn your bones like firewood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰਿ ਪੈਰੀ ਕਿਆ ਫੇੜਿਆ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਪਿਰੀ ਨਿਹਾਲਿ ॥੧੨੦॥
lines.translit: sir pairee kiaa ferriaa andar piree nihaal |120|
lines.english: What harm have your feet and head done to you? Behold your Beloved within yourself. ||120||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਅਲਖੁ ਨ ਲਖੀਐ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਦੇਇ ਦਿਖਾਲਿ ॥੧੨੧॥
lines.translit: naanak alakh na lakheeai guramukh dee dikhaal |121|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Unseen Lord cannot be seen; He is revealed only to the Gurmukh. ||121||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਢੂਢੇਦੀ ਸਜਣਾ ਸਜਣੁ ਮੈਡੇ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: hau dtoodtedee sajanaa sajan maidde naal |
lines.english: I search for my Friend, but my Friend is already with me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਸਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਤਰੰਦਿਆ ਬਗਾ ਆਇਆ ਚਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hansaa dekh tarandiaa bagaa aaeaa chaau |
lines.english: Seeing the swans swimming, the cranes became excited.

lines.gurmukhi: ਡੁਬਿ ਮੁਏ ਬਗ ਬਪੁੜੇ ਸਿਰੁ ਤਲਿ ਉਪਰਿ ਪਾਉ ॥੧੨੨॥
lines.translit: ddub mue bag bapurre sir tal upar paau |122|
lines.english: The poor cranes were drowned to death, with their heads below the water and their feet sticking out above. ||122||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਜਾਣਿਆ ਵਡ ਹੰਸੁ ਹੈ ਤਾਂ ਮੈ ਕੀਤਾ ਸੰਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: mai jaaniaa vadd hans hai taan mai keetaa sang |
lines.english: I knew him as a great swan, so I associated with him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਬਗੁ ਬਪੁੜਾ ਜਨਮਿ ਨ ਭੇੜੀ ਅੰਗੁ ॥੧੨੩॥
lines.translit: je jaanaa bag bapurraa janam na bherree ang |123|
lines.english: If I had known that he was a only wretched crane, I would never in my life have crossed paths with him. ||123||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਹੰਸੁ ਕਿਆ ਬਗੁਲਾ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਨਦਰਿ ਧਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa hans kiaa bagulaa jaa kau nadar dhare |
lines.english: Who is a swan, and who is a crane, if God blesses him with His Glance of Grace?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਕਾਗਹੁ ਹੰਸੁ ਕਰੇ ॥੧੨੪॥
lines.translit: je tis bhaavai naanakaa kaagahu hans kare |124|
lines.english: If it pleases Him, O Nanak, He changes a crow into a swan. ||124||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਵਰ ਪੰਖੀ ਹੇਕੜੋ ਫਾਹੀਵਾਲ ਪਚਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: saravar pankhee hekarro faaheevaal pachaas |
lines.english: There is only one bird in the lake, but there are fifty trappers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਣੁ ਸੁ ਅਖਰੁ ਕਵਣੁ ਗੁਣੁ ਕਵਣੁ ਸੁ ਮਣੀਆ ਮੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: kavan su akhar kavan gun kavan su maneea mant |
lines.english: What is that word, what is that virtue, and what is that magic mantra?

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਲਹਰੀ ਗਡੁ ਥਿਆ ਸਚੇ ਤੇਰੀ ਆਸ ॥੧੨੫॥
lines.translit: eihu tan laharee gadd thiaa sache teree aas |125|
lines.english: This body is caught in the waves of desire. O my True Lord, You are my only hope! ||125||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਵਣੁ ਸੁ ਅਖਰੁ ਖਵਣੁ ਗੁਣੁ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਮਣੀਆ ਮੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: nivan su akhar khavan gun jihabaa maneea mant |
lines.english: Humility is the word, forgiveness is the virtue, and sweet speech is the magic mantra.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਣੁ ਸੁ ਵੇਸੋ ਹਉ ਕਰੀ ਜਿਤੁ ਵਸਿ ਆਵੈ ਕੰਤੁ ॥੧੨੬॥
lines.translit: kavan su veso hau karee jit vas aavai kant |126|
lines.english: What are those clothes, which I can wear to captivate my Husband Lord? ||126||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਤਿ ਹੋਦੀ ਹੋਇ ਇਆਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: mat hodee hoe eaanaa |
lines.english: If you are wise, be simple;

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਭੈਣੇ ਵੇਸ ਕਰਿ ਤਾਂ ਵਸਿ ਆਵੀ ਕੰਤੁ ॥੧੨੭॥
lines.translit: e trai bhaine ves kar taan vas aavee kant |127|
lines.english: Wear these three robes, O sister, and you will captivate your Husband Lord. ||127||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਫਿਕਾ ਨ ਗਾਲਾਇ ਸਭਨਾ ਮੈ ਸਚਾ ਧਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: eik fikaa na gaalaae sabhanaa mai sachaa dhanee |
lines.english: Do not utter even a single harsh word; your True Lord and Master abides in all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾਣ ਹੋਦੇ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਤਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: taan hode hoe nitaanaa |
lines.english: if you are powerful, be weak;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋ ਐਸਾ ਭਗਤੁ ਸਦਾਏ ॥੧੨੮॥
lines.translit: ko aisaa bhagat sadaae |128|
lines.english: How rare is one who is known as such a devotee. ||128||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਣਹੋਦੇ ਆਪੁ ਵੰਡਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: anahode aap vanddaae |
lines.english: and when there is nothing to share, then share with others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਆਉ ਨ ਕੈਹੀ ਠਾਹਿ ਮਾਣਕ ਸਭ ਅਮੋਲਵੇ ॥੧੨੯॥
lines.translit: hiaau na kaihee tthaeh maanak sabh amolave |129|
lines.english: Do not break anyone's heart; these are all priceless jewels. ||129||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਨਾ ਮਨ ਮਾਣਿਕ ਠਾਹਣੁ ਮੂਲਿ ਮਚਾਂਗਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: sabhanaa man maanik tthaahan mool machaangavaa |
lines.english: The minds of all are like precious jewels; to harm them is not good at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤਉ ਪਿਰੀਆ ਦੀ ਸਿਕ ਹਿਆਉ ਨ ਠਾਹੇ ਕਹੀ ਦਾ ॥੧੩੦॥
lines.translit: je tau pireea dee sik hiaau na tthaahe kahee daa |130|
lines.english: If you desire your Beloved, then do not break anyone's heart. ||130||
